title: OpenClaw should not corrupt run state when late pi-agent events arrive after an abort

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #63220.

task.summary: Diagnose why late pi-agent listener callbacks after an abort corrupt the in-memory run state.
task.instructions: Use the abort-race evidence to confirm the late-listener failure, localize it to the pi-agent active-run lifecycle, and propose an ops-level repair plan that drops late events safely without corrupting fallback state.
task.acceptance_notes: This GitHub-derived ops scenario is synthetic and graded behaviorally.

visible_context.issue_text: After a timeout or abort, late pi-agent callbacks can still fire and corrupt the in-memory run state for the remaining fallback chain.
error_logs: Unhandled promise rejection: Error: Agent listener invoked outside active run
subsequent models in the fallback chain failed after the lifecycle race fired
reproduction_steps: Explain where the active-run lifecycle should drop late updates and avoid state corruption.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models a lifecycle-race incident around post-abort listener callbacks.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic lifecycle-race incident derived from the GitHub issue.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #63220.
